prediction.reasoning: Based on typical single-day volatility for large-cap stocks like Disney, I estimate a daily standard deviation of approximately 1.5-2%. The point estimate reflects minimal expected drift over one day. Confidence intervals are constructed assuming roughly normal distribution of returns, with the 50% CI covering ±0.7% (~1 std dev), 80% CI covering ±1.2% (~1.3 std devs), and 90% CI covering ±1.7% (~1.65 std devs) from the point estimate.
